message.content:
You are a skeptical evidence auditor (/er style — zero tolerance for assertions without raw proof). Audit the evidence bundle at /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703 (files: EVIDENCE.md, 01_init.txt..08_status_after_job.txt, config.toml, checksums.sha256).
Checks: (1) run `cd /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703 && sha256sum -c checksums.sha256` — any failure is a critical finding. (2) Circular provenance: is any claim supported only by an artifact the claim itself generated, or do independent artifacts corroborate (e.g. container ID in 04_status_before_job.txt vs hostname in 07_job_log.txt — these come from different systems: local docker vs GitHub's job log)? (3) Internal consistency: runner name, container id, limits (5977MB=6267338752 bytes? do the math), timestamps ordering. (4) Do the artifacts actually show what EVIDENCE.md claims, line by line? (5) Anything that smells fabricated or copy-pasted. Report findings with severity; if the bundle is sound say so explicitly in the summary.

input.description: List bundle files and verify checksums

content.content:
1	# ez-gh-actions E2E Evidence — real ephemeral runner, real GitHub Actions job
2	
3	- Repo: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions
4	- Commit under test: 5ef5406779925598565c8d277d13b865db78947a (fix c27d389 applied mid-run after E2E caught the daemon-capacity bug)
5	- Actions run (REAL, on our runner): https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28685531107
6	- Host: Jeff-Ubuntu (linux x86_64, 32 cpus/64GB), docker daemon = 4-cpu/12GB Lima VM
7	
8	## Proof chain
9	1. `01_init.txt` — ezgha init: daemon-aware limits (5977 MB / 2 cpus / 512 pids)
10	2. `02_doctor.txt` — capability detection (docker ok, kvm ok, virsh/tart/sysbox missing, gh auth ok)
11	3. `03_start.txt` — started ephemeral runner ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b
12	4. `04_status_before_job.txt` — container 1143cf62e74f running; GitHub runner #3 status=online
13	5. `06_run_watch.txt` — workflow_dispatch run 28685531107 completed success
14	6. `07_job_log.txt` — job output: runner name = ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b; job hostname = 1143cf62e74f (== our container ID); memory.max = 6267338752 (= 5977 MB exactly); pids.max = 512
15	7. `08_status_after_job.txt` — 0 containers, 0 registered runners (ephemeral: JIT deregistered + --rm cleanup)
16	
17	## Bug found and fixed by this E2E
18	`docker run --cpus 16` failed: daemon (Lima VM) has 4 cpus while host has 32.
19	Fix c27d389: limits derived from `docker info` NCPU/MemTotal + clamped at start.
20	Failure-path cleanup verified: the orphaned JIT registration was auto-removed.
21

input.command: cd /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions && git log --oneline -8 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; git cat-file -t 5ef5406779925598565c8d277d13b865db78947a 2>&1; git log --format='%h %s' -1 c27d389 2>&1
input.description: Check claimed commits exist in ez-gh-actions repo

input.command: gh api repos/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28685531107 --jq '{id, name, event, status, conclusion, head_sha, created_at, updated_at, run_started_at}' 2>&1
input.description: Verify GitHub Actions run 28685531107 exists and matches claims

content.content:
537e1e5 docs(evidence): E2E proof bundle — run 28685531107 on ezgha ephemeral runner
c27d389 fix: derive resource limits from docker daemon capacity, not host
5ef5406 feat: ez-gh-actions v1 — isolated ephemeral GitHub Actions runners
---
commit
c27d389 fix: derive resource limits from docker daemon capacity, not host

content.text: Live API confirms the run. Let me also verify the runner name from GitHub's job record independently, and double-check the byte math.

input.command: gh api repos/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28685531107/jobs --jq '.jobs[] | {name, runner_name, conclusion, started_at, completed_at, labels}' 2>&1; echo "---math---"; python3 -c "print(5977*1024*1024); print(11955//2)"
input.description: Verify job runner name via GitHub API and check MB-to-bytes math

findings.title: sha256sum -c fails: git_sha.txt listed in checksums.sha256 but missing from bundle
findings.detail: Running `cd /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703 && sha256sum -c checksums.sha256` exits 1 with `git_sha.txt: FAILED open or read`. The checksum manifest lists 11 files but only 10 exist. git_sha.txt (hash be8f6a12...) was presumably the artifact pinning the bundle to commit 5ef5406779925598565b78947a and was never committed or was deleted after the manifest was written. Per /er rules, any checksum failure caps the bundle at INVALID/incomplete regardless of other quality. Mitigation found during audit: the live GitHub API (repos/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28685531107) independently reports head_sha=5ef5406779925598565b78947a... matching EVIDENCE.md's claimed commit, so the underlying claim is TRUE — but the bundle itself does not carry that proof and fails its own integrity check.
findings.recommendation: Restore git_sha.txt (content: the commit SHA, e.g. output of `git rev-parse HEAD` at capture time) so its hash matches be8f6a12f958fa19c8e55b30ceddbda44f5374d14236c1fad6bd297ff7b87622, or regenerate checksums.sha256 to match the actual file set and re-verify. Do not ship a bundle whose manifest references absent files.

findings.title: 'Failure-path cleanup verified' and the --cpus 16 bug narrative have zero supporting artifacts in the bundle
findings.detail: EVIDENCE.md's 'Bug found and fixed by this E2E' section claims (a) `docker run --cpus 16` failed against the 4-cpu Lima daemon and (b) 'the orphaned JIT registration was auto-removed'. No artifact captures the failed docker run, the orphaned runner registration, or its removal — the bundle only contains the happy-path run after fix c27d389. Commit c27d389 ('fix: derive resource limits from docker daemon capacity, not host') does exist in the repo, corroborating the narrative indirectly, but the failure-path cleanup claim is assertion-only within the bundle.
findings.recommendation: Either attach the failure-path artifacts (error output of the failed start, a status snapshot showing the orphaned registration, and the post-cleanup 0-runner state) or soften EVIDENCE.md to say the cleanup was observed but not captured.

findings.title: The 2-cpu limit is not proven inside the container; job log nproc shows 4
findings.detail: config.toml and 01_init.txt claim `cpus = 2.0` per runner, but 07_job_log.txt's environment proof prints nproc=4 (the Lima daemon's CPU count) and captures no cpu.max cgroup file. Docker's --cpus is a CFS quota that nproc cannot see, so this is consistent — not a contradiction — but it means the in-container evidence corroborates only 2 of the 3 limits (memory.max 6267338752 = 5977 MiB exact, pids.max 512) while the cpu limit is unverified. EVIDENCE.md carefully avoids claiming the log proves cpus, but a casual reader of the 'limits' claim would assume all three were demonstrated.
findings.recommendation: Add `cat /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.max` to the selftest workflow's proof step (expected output '200000 100000' for cpus=2.0) so all three limits are demonstrated from inside the job.

input.summary: Bundle at /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703 is SUBSTANTIVELY SOUND but FAILS its own integrity check: sha256sum -c exits 1 because git_sha.txt is listed in checksums.sha256 but absent (critical — the one artifact pinning the bundle to the commit under test is missing). Everything else verified, including against live independent sources: (1) all 10 present files pass checksums; (2) provenance is NOT circular — container ID 1143cf62e74f appears both in local docker status (04) and as Machine name/hostname in GitHub's server-side job log (07), and I independently confirmed via the live GitHub API that run 28685531107 exists (workflow_dispatch, success, head_sha=5ef5406... exactly matching the claimed commit) and that GitHub's jobs endpoint reports runner_name ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b with labels [self-hosted, ezgha]; (3) internal math is exact — 5977 MiB * 1048576 = 6,267,338,752 = memory.max in the job log, and 11955 MB daemon / 2 = 5977 matches the daemon-derived-limits design; pids.max 512 matches config and log; timestamps order correctly (run 22:12:21Z → job log 22:12:26-27Z → bundle written ~22:14 UTC); commits 5ef5406 and c27d389 both exist in the repo with matching messages; (4) EVIDENCE.md claims map to artifacts line-by-line except the unbacked 'failure-path cleanup verified' narrative (minor) and the cpu limit, which nproc=4 cannot prove (minor — memory and pids limits ARE proven in-container); (5) nothing smells fabricated — kernel build date day-of-week checks out and the live-API cross-checks would be nearly impossible to fake. Verdict: fix the git_sha.txt checksum failure and this is a PASS-quality bundle; as shipped, the integrity failure caps it at PARTIAL.
